Active Directory Domain
Domain Controller · Domain-Joined Client · Group Policy █
// about

Standing up a Windows Server 2025 domain controller in VirtualBox, joining a Windows 11 client to it, and applying Group Policy to bring the environment in line with basic domain security practice.

jflaum.cyber192.168.10.0/24DC01 — 192.168.10.10Win11 — 192.168.10.20
// windows server 2025 domain controller

Create the Virtual Machine step 1

Downloaded the Windows Server 2025 evaluation ISO from Microsoft and created a new VM in VirtualBox.

  • Named the VM "Windows Server 2025"
  • Allocated 2048 MB RAM and 1 CPU
  • Created an 80 GB virtual disk
downloading the Windows Server 2025 evaluation ISO
naming the VM and pointing VirtualBox at the ISO
allocating memory and CPU
creating the 80GB virtual hard disk
unattended install setup — initial hostname and credentials

Install Windows Server 2025 step 2

  • Selected the Standard Evaluation (Desktop Experience) image
  • Selected the destination partition and let the unattended install run
  • Logged into the desktop once setup finished
selecting the Desktop Experience image
selecting the install destination
confirming install options before setup begins
first boot into the Windows Server 2025 desktop

Rename the Server & Set a Static IP step 3

  • Renamed the computer from its default name to DC01
  • Set a static internal IP with itself as the DNS server
  • Verified the configuration with ipconfig
renaming the computer to DC01
configuring a static IPv4 address and DNS server
C:\Users\Administrator> ipconfig Ethernet adapter Ethernet: IPv4 Address. . . . . . . . . . . : 192.168.10.10 Subnet Mask . . . . . . . . . . . : 255.255.255.0 Default Gateway . . . . . . . . . :
confirming the static IP took effect

Install AD DS & Promote to a Domain Controller step 4

  • Added the Active Directory Domain Services role via Server Manager
  • Promoted DC01 to a domain controller and created a new forest, jflaum.cyber
  • Set the forest/domain functional level to Windows Server 2025 and enabled DNS
  • Set a DSRM password and let the server restart to complete promotion
adding roles and features from Server Manager
selecting DC01 as the destination server
selecting the AD DS role and its required management tools
installing AD DS, GPMC, and the AD admin tools
post-deployment notification to promote the server
choosing "Add a new forest" — jflaum.cyber
setting the forest/domain functional level and DSRM password
DC01 restarting to finish the promotion
logging back in as JFLAUM\Administrator after promotion

Verify the Domain & Create a User step 5

  • Confirmed DC01 joined the jflaum.cyber domain after reboot
  • Opened Active Directory Users and Computers
  • Created the first domain user account, John Doe (jdoe)
C:\Users\Administrator> systeminfo | findstr /B /C:"Domain" Domain: jflaum.cyber
verifying domain membership
opening Active Directory Users and Computers
creating a new user object in jflaum.cyber
setting the name and logon name for John Doe (jdoe)
setting the account password
confirming the domain is live and reachable from the command line
Windows Server 2025AD DSDNSNew Forest
// windows 11 domain-joined client

Joining the Client to the Domain

Built a Windows 11 VM on the same internal network as DC01 so it could authenticate against Active Directory.

  • Built a Windows 11 VM on the 192.168.10.0/24 network alongside DC01
  • Set its static IP to 192.168.10.20 and pointed DNS at DC01 (192.168.10.10) so it could locate the domain
  • Joined the client to jflaum.cyber via System Properties and rebooted to complete the join
  • Logged in with the jdoe domain account created on DC01 to confirm authentication against Active Directory
Note: DNS has to point at the domain controller before the join — pointing at a public DNS server first is the most common reason a domain join fails.
Windows 11Domain JoinDNS
// group policy

Group Policy & Security Hardening

GPO design, OU/group structure, and the full set of hardening policies (password policy, screen lock, Defender AV, firewall, AppLocker, USB blocking, audit logging) got involved enough to split into its own write-up.

// view Group Policy & Security Hardening lab →

// what i learned
  • How an unattended VirtualBox install differs from a manual Windows setup
  • Why a domain controller needs a static IP and to point DNS at itself
  • The full sequence behind "promote to domain controller" — forest type, functional level, DSRM password, and the required restart
  • How Group Policy is authored on the DC and applied to joined clients
// jordan flaum  ·  ← back to home lab
